message:
feat(wal): expand per-core WAL buffer with parallel flush, contention metrics, and E2E pilot

Per-core buffer (593 new lines):
- Implement parallel flush coordination across per-core WAL buffers
  with epoch-based synchronization barriers
- Add contention metrics collection (lock wait histograms, buffer
  saturation counters) for runtime observability
- Expand buffer lifecycle management with graceful drain-on-shutdown
  and crash-recovery replay from partial flush state

Supporting changes:
- Design doc: add 16 lines documenting parallel flush architecture
  decisions and ordering guarantees
- E2E pilot script: expand test scenarios (61 lines) covering
  concurrent writer stress, mid-flush crash injection, and
  cross-process recovery validation
- verify_e2e_log_schema.sh: fix schema path resolution
- Add ncivz_1 parallel WAL buffer pilot JSONL artifact
- Sync beads issue tracker metadata

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -1136,7 +1136,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-n3p4","title":"Corruption Models: Define realistic corruption modes + safety rails","description":"## Goal
Define the corruption modes we will simulate and how to keep them safe/reproducible.

## Corruption Modes (Initial)
- **Bit flips**: flip N random bits/bytes (seeded).
- **Page zeroing**: overwrite a full page-aligned 4KB chunk with zeros.
- **Truncation**: truncate file by M bytes / to page boundary.
- **WAL tail loss** (if WAL present): truncate `-wal` after K frames.

## Safety Rails
- Operate only on `work/` copies.
- Always record seed + offsets mutated.
- Keep pre-corruption copy to compare.

## Acceptance
- Documented corruption spec and how each will be detected (checksums + sqlite integrity_check expectations).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:22:09.929714225Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T04:40:53.264120210Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T04:40:53.264100463Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of existing E2E suite tasks; superseded by canonical bd-z0xm subgraph.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["durability","e2e","repair"],"comments":[{"id":2887,"issue_id":"bd-n3p4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Implementation matches the task scope and title
- All unit tests pass; code compiles without warnings under clippy pedantic+nursery
- Integration with existing harness framework verified
- No unsafe code, no panics in normal operation
","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:22:38Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-ncivz","title":"Parallel WAL — Silo/Aether Style (§15.4)","description":"Durability scaling for concurrent writers via parallel logging. The current single WAL is a serialization bottleneck — all concurrent writers must serialize their WAL appends. Score 5.3 in the opportunity matrix. Critical for the concurrent-writer story that page-level MVCC enables.

## Background
SQLite's WAL uses a single-writer model (WAL_WRITE_LOCK). FrankenSQLite's page-level MVCC allows concurrent writers at the MVCC layer, but the WAL becomes the new bottleneck if it remains single-writer.

## Key Papers
- Tu, Zheng, Kohler, Liskov, Madden 2013: 'Speedy Transactions in Multicore In-Memory Databases' (Silo — epoch-based parallel logging)
- Johnson, Pandis, Stoica, Athanassoulis, Ailamaki 2010: 'Aether: A Scalable Approach to Logging' (consolidation-based group commit)

## Architecture
Silo-style parallel logging:
1. Per-core log buffers: each worker thread writes to its own log buffer (no contention)
2. Epoch-based ordering: global epoch advances periodically. All log records within an epoch are equivalent for ordering purposes.
3. Epoch boundary = durability boundary: at each epoch advance, all log buffers are flushed to persistent storage
4. Recovery: replay all log buffers in epoch order (within an epoch, order does not matter for committed transactions)

Aether-style group commit with consolidation:
1. Writers add their log records to a consolidation buffer
2. A designated 'flusher' thread (or the first writer in a group) performs the actual I/O
3. All writers in the group are notified when the flush completes
4. Amortizes fsync overhead across multiple transactions

Combined approach:
- Per-core buffers for zero-contention logging
- Periodic epoch advance triggers consolidation and group flush
- Single fsync per epoch covers all writers

## Expected Impact
- Write throughput scales linearly with cores (currently bottlenecked at 1 core)
- Group commit reduces fsync overhead (dominant cost for small transactions)
- Latency: individual transaction commit latency bounded by epoch interval (configurable, default 10ms)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Per-core WAL buffers with zero-contention writes
- [ ] Epoch-based ordering across buffers
- [ ] Group commit with consolidation
- [ ] Recovery correctly replays multi-buffer WAL
- [ ] Write throughput scales with cores
- [ ] Commit latency bounded by epoch interval
- [ ] Benchmark: parallel WAL vs single WAL throughput

## Success Criteria
Epic completion requires all child beads to satisfy their structured acceptance criteria, including explicit unit/property coverage, deterministic e2e evidence, and structured logging/metrics validation.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Every child bead in this epic defines concrete unit/property test scope with deterministic pass/fail thresholds and invariant IDs. 2. Critical-path end-to-end scenarios are implemented as reproducible e2e scripts (fixed seeds, replay commands, artifact bundles) across happy-path and adversarial-path behavior. 3. Structured logging and metrics contracts are mandatory for all child outputs (trace_id, run_id, scenario_id, seed, timing, outcome, error context) and validated by CI conformance checks. 4.  {"id":"bd-ncivz.1","title":"Design per-core WAL buffer architecture","description":"Design the per-core WAL buffer architecture for parallel logging. Each worker thread has a dedicated log buffer that it writes to without any synchronization.

Design considerations:
1. Buffer allocation: pre-allocate per-core buffers at startup (size: configurable, default 4MB per core)
2. Buffer format: sequence of (txn_id, epoch, page_id, before_image, after_image) records
3. NUMA locality: allocate buffer memory on the same NUMA node as the core
4. Overflow handling: when a buffer is full before epoch advance, either block the writer or allocate overflow buffer
5. Thread-to-buffer mapping: use thread-local storage or core affinity to map threads to buffers
6. Buffer lifecycle: buffers are recycled after flush (double-buffering: write to buffer A while flushing buffer B)

Integration with MVCC:
- Each MVCC write generates a WAL record in the writer's per-core buffer
- The WAL record includes the MVCC version information (txn_id, begin_ts, end_ts)
- On commit, the transaction's WAL records are 'sealed' (marked as committed) in the buffer

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Design document with buffer format, allocation strategy, and lifecycle
- [ ] NUMA-aware allocation strategy documented
- [ ] Overflow handling policy specified
- [ ] Double-buffering scheme for concurrent write/flush
- [ ] Integration points with MVCC write path identified
- [ ] Prototype: per-core buffers with concurrent writers (no contention)","acceptance_criteria":"1. Design artifact defines state machine/data model, explicit invariants, failure modes, and deterministic fallback behavior with clear ownership boundaries. 2. Unit tests validate schema/state-transition invariants derived directly from the design specification. 3. A pilot end-to-end scenario proves the design contract in integrated execution and emits replayable artifacts. 4. Logging contract for the design specifies required structured fields and automated conformance checks to catch drift early.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:23:07.635963477Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T06:09:53.909075929Z","closed_at":"2026-02-16T06:09:53.909025064Z","close_reason":"Completed bd-ncivz.1 design artifact + per-core WAL prototype tests + deterministic e2e pilot evidence","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-concept","alien-pass-2026-02-13","design","durability","ev-gated"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ncivz.1","depends_on_id":"bd-ncivz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-ncivz.2","title":"Implement epoch-based ordering across per-core buffers","description":"Implement the epoch-based ordering mechanism that provides a total order across per-core WAL buffers. This is the key insight from Silo: within an epoch, the order of operations across cores does not matter for correctness.

Epoch management:
1. Global epoch counter: atomic u64, advanced periodically (configurable interval, default 10ms)
2. Epoch advance protocol: 
   a. Increment global epoch E_new = E_old + 1
   b. Wait for all active transactions to observe E_new (fence)
   c. All log records written before the fence are tagged with E_old
   d. Log records after the fence are tagged with E_new
3. Durability guarantee: when epoch E is flushed, all transactions that committed in epoch <= E are durable

Transaction commit protocol:
1. Writer seals log records in per-core buffer with current epoch
2. Writer waits for its epoch to be flushed (group commit)
3. Epoch flush: collect all sealed records from all per-core buffers for epoch E, write to persistent storage, fsync
4. Notify all waiting writers that epoch E is durable

Recovery ordering:
- Replay log records in epoch order (ascending)
- Within an epoch: apply all committed transactions (order does not matter due to MVCC isolation)
- Uncommitted transactions within an epoch: discard during recovery

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Global epoch counter with periodic advance
- [ ] Log records correctly tagged with epoch
- [ ] Group commit: flush all per-core buffers per epoch
- [ ] Writers correctly block until their epoch is durable
- [ ] Recovery replays in epoch order
- [ ] Correctness: concurrent transactions committed in different epochs maintain serial order
- [ ] Fence correctness: no log records straddling epoch boundaries","acceptance_criteria":"1. Implementation is complete and behaviorally correct against explicit invariants and edge-case requirements, including fault and recovery paths where applicable. 2. Comprehensive unit/property tests cover normal, boundary, and adversarial conditions with deterministic fixtures and regression seeds. 3. End-to-end scenario scripts validate integrated behavior in real execution flows and include deterministic replay instructions. 4. Structured logging/metrics (traceable IDs, timing, counters, error context) are emitted and validated so failures are diagnosable and auditable.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:23:21.492845647Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:06:14.253308357Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-concept","alien-pass-2026-02-13","durability","ev-gated","parallelism"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ncivz.2","depends_on_id":"bd-ncivz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ncivz.2","depends_on_id":"bd-ncivz.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-ncivz.3","title":"Implement group commit with consolidation","description":"Implement Aether-style group commit with consolidation to amortize fsync overhead across multiple transactions. fsync is the dominant cost for small transaction commits (1-10ms per call). Group commit batches multiple transactions into a single fsync.

Consolidation protocol:
1. Writers add their sealed log records to a consolidation queue
2. Consolidation phases: FILLING (accepting records) -> FLUSHING (writing to disk) -> COMPLETE (notify waiters)
3. The first writer to enter FILLING state becomes the 'flusher'
4. Subsequent writers add records and wait on a condition variable
5. Flusher: when epoch advance triggers, collect all records in FILLING state
6. Flusher: write consolidated batch to WAL file, fsync
7. Flusher: notify all waiting writers, transition to COMPLETE
8. Next epoch begins a new FILLING phase

I/O optimization:
- Consolidated write: single writev() syscall with scatter-gather of all per-core buffers
- io_uring for async write+fsync on Linux (with fallback to synchronous)
- Direct I/O to bypass page cache (WAL is write-once, page cache is wasted)

Tuning:
- Group size target: configurable (default: wait up to 1ms for additional writers before flushing)
- Maximum group delay: bounded to ensure tail latency (default: 10ms)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Consolidation protocol correctly batches multiple writers
- [ ] Single fsync per group commit
- [ ] writev() scatter-gather for efficient I/O
- [ ] io_uring integration (with fallback)
- [ ] Commit latency bounded by max group delay
- [ ] Benchmark: commits/second with group commit vs individual fsync
- [ ] Correctness: all committed transactions durable after group fsync","acceptance_criteria":"1. Implementation is complete and behaviorally correct against explicit invariants and edge-case requirements, including fault and recovery paths where applicable. 2. Comprehensive unit/property tests cover normal, boundary, and adversarial conditions with deterministic fixtures and regression seeds. 3. End-to-end scenario scripts validate integrated behavior in real execution flows and include deterministic replay instructions. 4. Structured logging/metrics (traceable IDs, timing, counters, error context) are emitted and validated so failures are diagnosable and auditable.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:23:34.204215921Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:06:13.927868157Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-concept","alien-pass-2026-02-13","durability","ev-gated","performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ncivz.3","depends_on_id":"bd-ncivz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ncivz.3","depends_on_id":"bd-ncivz.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-ncivz.4","title":"Benchmark: parallel WAL vs single WAL throughput","description":"Benchmark parallel WAL throughput against single-writer WAL to validate the scaling improvement. This is the key metric for the concurrent-writer story.

Workloads:
1. Small transactions (1 page write each): 1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 32 concurrent writers
2. Medium transactions (10 page writes each): same concurrency levels
3. Large transactions (100 page writes each): same concurrency levels
4. Mixed: 80% small + 15% medium + 5% large at 32 concurrent writers

Metrics:
- Transactions committed per second (the primary metric)
- Commit latency: p50, p95, p99
- WAL write throughput (MB/s)
- fsync frequency (fsyncs/s)
- CPU utilization per core
- I/O wait time

Expected results:
- Single WAL: throughput plateaus at 1-2 cores (fsync bottleneck)
- Parallel WAL: near-linear scaling to 8+ cores (group commit amortizes fsync)
- Group commit: 10-50x fsync reduction (from per-transaction to per-epoch)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 4 workloads benchmarked at all concurrency levels
- [ ] Scaling plot: throughput vs writer count
- [ ] Parallel WAL shows near-linear scaling to 8 cores
- [ ] Commit latency p99 bounded by epoch interval
- [ ] Group commit effectiveness quantified
- [ ] CI integration: regression detection on SF1 workload","acceptance_criteria":"1. Benchmark harness specifies workload, dataset shape, seed, and environment controls, and reports throughput plus p50/p95/p99 latency against a stable baseline. 2. Correctness guard tests (unit/property) run before performance assertions so regressions never trade correctness for speed. 3. An end-to-end smoke scenario validates the benchmarked path in integrated engine mode and verifies comparable outputs. 4. Benchmark logs include hardware/runtime config, query/workload IDs, sample distributions, and artifact paths for independent reproduction.  8. Unit/property test coverage for this bead is mandatory, including deterministic seeds and explicit invariant checks. 9. At least one deterministic end-to-end scenario script must validate integrated behavior with replay instructions and archived artifacts. 10. Structured logging/metrics validation is required: trace/run/scenario IDs, timing, counters, and actionable failure context must be present and CI-verified.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:23:43.937466723Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:08:51.536821705Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-concept","alien-pass-2026-02-13","benchmark","durability","ev-gated"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ncivz.4","depends_on_id":"bd-ncivz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ncivz.4","depends_on_id":"bd-ncivz.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ncivz.4","depends_on_id":"bd-ncivz.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ncivz.4","depends_on_id":"bd-ncivz.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-ncivz.5","title":"E2E test: crash recovery with parallel WAL multi-buffer replay","description":"End-to-end test validating crash recovery correctness when using the parallel WAL with per-core buffers. Parallel WAL recovery is more complex than single-WAL recovery because log records are distributed across multiple buffers and must be replayed in epoch order.

## Test Scenarios

1. Crash mid-epoch: Kill process during an active epoch (some per-core buffers flushed, others not). Expected: recovery replays all committed transactions from flushed buffers. Unflushed committed transactions may be lost (within the documented durability window).

2. Crash during epoch flush: Kill process while the group commit flusher is writing per-core buffers to disk. Expected: partial flush produces a valid WAL (each buffer flush is atomic via aligned writes).

3. Crash with mixed epoch records: Multiple epochs in the WAL (some fully flushed, latest partially flushed). Expected: all fully-flushed epochs recovered completely.

4. Crash with concurrent SSI aborts: Kill process while SSI abort is rolling back writes. Expected: recovery correctly identifies and discards uncommitted (aborted) transactions.

5. Multi-buffer ordering correctness: After recovery, verify that the recovered state matches what would result from serial replay of all committed transactions in epoch order.

## Alien CS Reference
Tu et al. 2013 Silo: Section 4.2 describes epoch-based recovery protocol for parallel logging. Zheng et al. 2014: recovery ordering guarantees for distributed log buffers. Johnson et al. 2010 Aether: group commit crash recovery semantics and partial-flush handling.

## Rust Implementation Guidance
- Use the fail crate for crash injection at specific code points in the WAL flush path
- Implement crash scenarios as integration tests with #[test] and fork: use std::process::Command to spawn child process, then kill with SIGKILL at injection point
- Use tempfile::TempDir for isolated test databases (cleanup on drop)
- Recovery verification: compute BLAKE3 checksum of database state post-recovery, compare against expected state from known committed transactions
- Use crossbeam::scope for concurrent writer threads in test setup

## Logging Requirements
- Log every crash injection point and the state at crash time at tracing::info level
- Log recovery replay decisions: which epochs replayed, which transactions recovered/discarded at tracing::info level
- Log final verification result with expected vs actual checksums at tracing::info level
- Log partial-flush detection and handling at tracing::warn level
- Log any data loss events (unflushed committed transactions) at tracing::warn level

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 5 crash scenarios implemented and passing
- [ ] Recovery produces consistent database state in all scenarios
- [ ] No committed data lost (within documented durability window)
- [ ] No uncommitted data visible after recovery
- [ ] Multi-buffer replay ordering verified deterministic
- [ ] 50 randomized crash points per scenario
- [ ] CI integration (nightly, due to time cost)","acceptance_criteria":"1. End-to-end scripts cover happy-path and adversarial-path behavior with deterministic seeds, one-command replay, and explicit oracle checks. 2. Companion unit tests validate scenario generators, checkers, and artifact validators to prevent false positives in e2e outcomes. 3. Structured logs include trace/run/scenario identifiers, timing, resource stats, and root-cause context sufficient for post-failure triage without re-instrumentation. 4. CI archives artifact bundles (logs, manifests, checksums) and fails on nondeterminism, missing artifacts, or schema violations.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:21:26.587680794Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T04:41:27.109888823Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-pass-2026-02-13","ev-gated"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ncivz.5","depends_on_id":"bd-ncivz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ncivz.5","depends_on_id":"bd-ncivz.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ncivz.5","depends_on_id":"bd-ncivz.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ncivz.5","depends_on_id":"bd-ncivz.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T04:41:27.109842816Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

+## `bd-ncivz.2` Progress Notes
+
+`bd-ncivz.2` builds directly on this contract with an epoch-order coordinator in
+`crates/fsqlite-wal/src/per_core_buffer.rs`:
+
+- global epoch clock with default `10ms` advance interval metadata
+- active-core fence (`advance_epoch_and_wait`) before sealing the previous epoch
+- per-epoch group flush across all core lanes with deterministic straddle detection
+- durability wait API (`wait_until_epoch_durable`) for writer unblock semantics
+- recovery ordering helper that replays records by `(epoch, begin_seq, txn_id, page_id)`
+

+                echo "ERROR: --filter requires a value" >&2
+                exit 2
+            fi

+            shift 2
+            ;;

+                echo "ERROR: --scenario-id requires a value" >&2
+                exit 2

+            shift 2
+            ;;

+                echo "ERROR: --bead-id requires a value" >&2
+                exit 2
+            fi
+            BEAD_ID="$2"
+            shift 2
+            ;;
+        --seed)
+            if [[ $# -lt 2 ]]; then
+                echo "ERROR: --seed requires a value" >&2
+                exit 2
+            fi
+            SEED="$2"
+            shift 2
+            ;;
+        *)
+            echo "ERROR: unknown argument '$1'" >&2
+            exit 2
+            ;;

+done
+
